authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST publishes the public control catalog used across U.S. federal and commercial security programs; it is directly authoritative for control IDs, control families, enhancements, and control language used in mappings.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53A Rev. 5, Assessing Security and Privacy Controls
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST assessment procedures define recognized assessment objects and methods that constrain whether evidence can support a control assertion.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-37 Rev. 2, Risk Management Framework
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The RMF is the recognized public workflow around selecting, implementing, assessing, authorizing, and monitoring controls; it establishes where mapping support stops before authorization.
used_for: categorize, select, implement, assess, authorize, and monitor boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST CSF 2.0 is a public cross-sector cybersecurity framework that provides outcome-oriented operating language for organizations of all sizes and sectors.
used_for: Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, and Recover mapping

authoritative_sources.title: NIST IR 8278 Rev. 1, National Online Informative References Program
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST OLIR is specifically about standardized relationships between documents such as NIST CSF, Privacy Framework, and SP 800-53, making it directly authoritative for mapping lineage and relationship discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals Common Baseline
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA publishes cross-sector cybersecurity performance goals for IT and OT owners; this source supplies operational baseline language that materially changes mappings from generic control lists to cross-sector resilience expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: CIS Critical Security Controls Version 8
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIS maintains a recognized prioritized safeguard catalog and implementation-group model used by many organizations for practical security control implementation and measurement.

authoritative_sources.title: CIS Controls v8 Mapping to NIST CSF 2.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIS publishes an official mapping from its safeguards to NIST CSF 2.0, directly supporting mapping relationships and source-specific relationship constraints.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard 5.0.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized open application-security verification standard with versioned requirement IDs, making it authoritative for application-control mapping and identifier stability expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Information security management systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization and International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 is an international ISMS requirements standard commonly used as an enterprise control mapping target; certification claims remain outside this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the official payment card standards body; PCI DSS changes records, scope, evidence, and assessor authority when cardholder data environments are involved.

workflow_stages.name: scope and authority intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm system, data, environment, business criticality, framework versions, tenant policies, approval matrix, sector context, and target output before mapping begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: control and evidence normalization
workflow_stages.description: Normalize source controls, target controls, tenant policy clauses, evidence artifact IDs, owners, environment scope, inherited controls, and shared-responsibility assumptions.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: mapping, lineage, and confidence assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assign direct, partial, inherited, compensating, not-applicable, duplicate, or conflicting mapping relationships with confidence, rationale, source version, and evidence lineage.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: gap, defect, incident, and nonconformance handling
workflow_stages.description: Identify missing coverage, weak evidence, stale evidence, repeat defects, recurring incidents, open findings, and required corrective-action escalation without executing incident response or remediation.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: review-ready output and boundary gating
workflow_stages.description: Package the matrix, gap report, remediation backlog, quality or safety review note, evidence summary, hold recommendation, and escalation decisions while preserving final reviewer authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: system, application, platform, service, environment, and data-scope inventory
source and target framework versions, control catalogs, profiles, baselines, and implementation group assumptions
tenant policies, standards, procedures, contractual commitments, customer control addenda, and approval matrix
control evidence such as configuration exports, screenshots with provenance, tickets, runbooks, monitoring records, test records, incident summaries, defect logs, and exception records
control mapping matrix with lineage, coverage gap and overlap report, risk-prioritized remediation backlog, readiness evidence summary, review note, and corrective-action escalation
source freshness register, reviewer decision log, risk acceptance record, compensating-control statement, and unresolved ambiguity list
systems_of_record: GRC or integrated risk management platform
audit evidence repository or document management system
cloud, identity, CI/CD, logging, monitoring, and vulnerability evidence systems
ticketing, incident, defect, nonconformance, and corrective-action trackers
exception, risk acceptance, and compensating-control registers
tenant approval matrix and reviewer workflow

allowed: classify security control mapping requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
produce control mapping matrices, coverage analyses, evidence summaries, remediation backlogs, and review notes
recommend mapping relationships, evidence confidence, hold or release posture, and corrective-action escalation based on visible records
adapt public baselines to tenant-specific policy overlays while preserving assumptions and source lineage
route adjacent execution, signoff, legal, privacy, audit, incident response, architecture, or production authority to the correct owner
escalate: source, tenant policy, contract, jurisdiction, or sector guidance conflicts materially change the mapping answer
evidence is missing, stale, inaccessible, contradictory, or not traceable to the scoped system
industry-specific facts may affect records, safety controls, payment scope, privacy obligations, or approval authority
control closure, hold release, audit attestation, or compliance signoff requires an authorized reviewer
refuse: execute penetration tests, exploitation, scans, vulnerability validation, or adversary simulation
perform incident response containment, triage, eradication, recovery, or forensic workflows
provide formal audit attestation, certification claims, legal opinions, privacy determinations, or final compliance signoff
own broad architecture, SSO, platform migration, production remediation, operational runbook execution, or broad policy drafting
bypass hold, release, quality, safety, or control-owner approval boundaries

domain_failure_modes: a partial mapping is mislabeled as equivalent and creates false assurance
evidence appears complete but cannot be tied to the system, environment, owner, collection date, or control version
a tenant GRC mapping overrides a public standard conflict without reviewer adjudication
a payment, healthcare, public-sector, OT, privacy, or contractual sector fact materially changes evidence requirements but is ignored as cosmetic
a hold, finding, or quality gate is cleared without reviewer evidence and authority
incident summaries are converted into incident response execution or forensic conclusions
penetration testing or vulnerability validation is performed under the control mapping lane
formal audit, certification, or compliance language is generated from mapping evidence alone
stale framework, OLIR, CIS, ASVS, PCI, ISO, or tenant policy versions are used for confident mapping
shared-responsibility controls in multi-tenant SaaS are assigned to the wrong owner

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Industry-specific prerequisite or evidence gap
Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
normal in-scope analysis
edge-case analysis with partial evidence
prioritization and recommendation quality
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
clear evidence-backed rationale
no unreviewed regressions
Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding.
Repeat defect or recurring incident.
Request to bypass a documented safety or quality control.
Scenario proving industry-specific in-scope execution.
Scenario proving escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources cannot determine a tenant's contract, jurisdiction, sector scope, data classification, payment scope, or reviewer authority at generation time.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Runtime must retrieve or ask for tenant facts and escalate when missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Control mapping evidence can support a reviewer packet, but public standards reserve final certification, audit, and assessor conclusions for authorized parties.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: The specialist must refuse formal attestation and route to the proper authority.
